questions.prompt: It has been several months since the incident response plan was reviewed. What is the most effective way to stay prepared?

options.label: A
options.text: Run a short tabletop exercise with key staff

options.label: B
options.text: Email the document to staff to read it in their own time

options.label: C
options.text: Assume the plan will work without testing

questions.prompt: A competitor recently experienced a large-scale service outage due to a DoS attack. How should your organisation respond?

options.label: A
options.text: Review what happened and assess your own exposure

options.label: B
options.text: Acknowledge it but take no action

options.label: C
options.text: Assume your organisation is too small to be targeted

questions.prompt: Industry alerts indicate increased DoS activity targeting your sector. What should leadership do?

options.label: A
options.text: Confirm IT is aware and protective measures are in place

questions.prompt: During a meeting, no one is sure where the response procedures are stored. What is the correct approach?

options.label: A
options.text: Ensure updated procedures are centrally accessible

options.label: B
options.text: Keep copies on individual managers’ devices

options.label: C
options.text: Rely on memory instead of documentation

questions.prompt: You want to detect unusual traffic patterns early. What preparation helps us most?

options.label: A
options.text: Understand what normal traffic levels look like

options.label: B
options.text: Only review systems during a crisis

questions.prompt: Employees are unfamiliar with how service disruptions should be reported. What improves readiness?

options.label: A
options.text: Train staff on how to recognise and report service issues

options.label: C
options.text: Assume staff will know what to do

questions.prompt: If email systems go down during an attack, communication may fail. What is the safest preparation?

options.label: B
options.text: Use the same systems even during outage

options.label: C
options.text: Decide how to communicate during the crisis

questions.prompt: Customers report that your website is suddenly unreachable. What is your first action?

options.label: B
options.text: Wait to see if the issue resolves

questions.prompt: Staff report slow systems, website timeouts, and repeated disconnects. What should you assume?

options.label: A
options.text: Treat it as a potential security issue and escalate

options.label: B
options.text: Assume it is a routine maintenance

questions.prompt: Monitoring tools generate high traffic alerts overnight. What is the correct response?
Traffic spikes continue and services degrade further. What should leadership do?

options.label: A
options.text: Activate the Cyber Incident Response Team

questions.prompt: Multiple departments report disruption. What information is most useful?

options.label: A
options.text: Record time, systems affected, and impact

questions.prompt: Service disruption exceeds acceptable downtime limits. What should happen?

options.label: B
options.text: Decide escalation based on personal judgement

questions.prompt: The attack appears coordinated and sustained. What is the prudent action?

options.label: A
options.text: Consider engaging specialists to analyse traffic patterns

options.label: C
options.text: Assume no further investigation is required

questions.prompt: Business leaders are unsure whether this is malicious or technical failure. What should you do?
You suspect systems may be intentionally overwhelmed. What needs confirmation?

options.label: A
options.text: Whether systems are under deliberate attack

options.label: B
options.text: Assume it is routine traffic growth

questions.prompt: Service outages spread to additional platforms. What should be assessed?

options.label: A
options.text: Business impact and whether other systems are targeted

questions.prompt: You need clarity on how events unfolded. What helps most?

options.label: A
options.text: Document when issues started and escalated

questions.prompt: Technical teams suggest different attack patterns. What should leadership request?

options.label: A
options.text: Clear explanation of attack type and risks

questions.prompt: Security teams request deeper traffic analysis. What is the correct response?

options.label: A
options.text: Approve detailed analysis to understand the attack

questions.prompt: Logs may help identify the attack source. What should be done?

options.label: A
options.text: Preserve and share logs securely with investigators

options.label: B
options.text: Delete old logs to free storage

questions.prompt: There are concerns the attack may be distracting from data theft. What is safest?

options.label: A
options.text: Consult the Data Protection Officer to assess risk

questions.prompt: Traffic is overwhelming servers. What immediate step can reduce impact?

options.label: A
options.text: Work with ISP to block malicious traffic

options.label: B
options.text: Wait for traffic to subside naturally

questions.prompt: Core services remain unavailable. What should leadership consider?
Attack traffic continues at high levels. What helps mitigate damage?

options.label: A
options.text: Apply traffic filtering at network edge

questions.prompt: Technical teams propose multiple mitigation steps. What should you do?
Sensitive systems are exposed online. What reduces risk?

options.label: A
options.text: Restrict access to approved IP ranges

questions.prompt: Vulnerabilities may have enabled the attack. What is the best response?
Traffic levels return to normal. How should services be restored?
The attack has ended. What documentation is required?
Leadership wants to prevent recurrence. What is the most effective?

options.label: A
options.text: Review response and update controls formally

options.label: C
options.text: Assume the issue will not repeat

questions.prompt: Employees experienced service disruption. What should be done?

options.label: A
options.text: Educate staff on what happened and how to report issues

questions.prompt: Weaknesses were identified during the response. What is the appropriate step?

options.label: A
options.text: Update response documentation and test it
